steps.title: Identify the logical gap the question exploits
steps.content:
The passage states that historians of capitalism rely on archival records, which do not exist for much of precolonial Africa. Yet Alfagali and other Africanist scholars have demonstrated that capitalism did exist in precolonial Africa. If the standard evidence base is unavailable, those scholars must have used something else.

Now check each choice against that inference:
  • Choice A — They likely make use of different types of evidence than historians of capitalism typically rely on.: Since archival records don't exist for precolonial Africa, Alfagali must have drawn on non-archival sources. This follows directly from the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — They likely differ from historians of capitalism in the methods they use to derive longitudinal economic data from archival records.: The passage says those archival records simply do not exist for precolonial Africa, so Alfagali could not have used them by any method. Contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — They likely have a different view about which activities should be considered capitalist in nature than historians of capitalism do.: The text treats "returns-focused investment" and "open markets for wage labor" as uncontested features of capitalism. No definitional disagreement is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — They likely view capitalism as having been more beneficial for Africa than historians of capitalism do.: The passage discusses the existence of capitalism, not its effects or benefits. No evaluative comparison is made. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only the matching choice is logically supported: Alfagali's findings about precolonial Africa require evidence beyond the archival records that other historians depend on. A is correct.
